channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Erenshor Celebrates First Anniversary With a Look Back, and Info on What's in the Next Patch
item.content:encoded:
Erenshor, the single-player simulated MMORPG, is celebrating its first anniversary, so the game’s dev is looking back and telling us what’s next.

In a blog on the game’s Steam page, solo developer Brian is talking about launch, what’s next, and how a laggy internet connection and falling in love with MMORPGs helped inspire the Erenshor we now know. 

And, perhaps, there are plenty of us who have had similar experiences at one point or another. “Back then, I didn’t really have internet service capable of serious play - I always found myself either kicked from groups or forcefully removed by lag. My playtime was forced to become more about exploring, looking for secrets, learning the lore, and just existing in that world. I would idolize all the high level players and their awesome gear and wonder where they got it,” he says.

With that, if you've been following anything about Erenshor, and how it takes the MMORPG experience and tunes it for a single-player experience, then it clicks immediately.  Thanks to the community's support, developing the game is now a full-time job, and updates will continue.

Brian says he started making some version of the game about a dozen times before starting in 2021 on what would eventually become the released versions of Erenshor. 

You can follow along with how things have happened, including how the community got involved and continued to inspire changes, help to volunteer to test the game and help it get over the finish line.

As for what’s next, community input is still important, and while there hasn't been an update for a little while, the next one will add raids. We get this info to close out this brand new blog, with a note that the testers are getting this patch this week and we should see it soon.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/erenshor-celebrates-first-anniversary-with-a-look-back-and-info-on-whats-in-the-next-patch-2000137806
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sky: Children of the Light's New Update Gets More Social With 'Presence' and Multiplayer Memory Features
item.content:encoded:
Sky: Children of the Light is introducing features to make the social MMO adventure feel even more social.

A new “Presence” feature will give more of an “MMO” feel to the MMO adventure. While Sky already allows players to linger together in the same instance, my estimation based on what's shown so far is that swapping on Presence loads in the, well, “presences” of other Sky players that are in the same zone, but on other instances, filling out the zone better and allowing better linking up with those other players. 

There’ll be a limited release of the feature in the Aviary Village hub to some players, with rollout taking place over time. More details on it are on the way. 

In a similar vein, Sky is introducing multiplayer Memories. Now instead of the shared Memories only permitting one person, multiple players can show up in a Shared Memory together, allowing for possible shenanigans (or whole bands of musicians, possibly). 

There are also general improvements made to candlewax collection, allowing players to see how much is collectible, and general environmental interactions are easier. The “Constellation” friends list feature is getting improvements as well, allowing a full-on list mode, including multi-friend selection, for better friend management. 

According to the monthly calendar, this update is out today, April 14. Running right now in Sky is the Earth Day event, “Days of Nature,” and the latest seasonal arc, Season of Carnival, is slated for an April 17 release. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/sky-children-of-the-lights-new-update-gets-more-social-with-presence-and-multiplayer-memory-features-2000137798
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Learn More About Eterspire's Unlucky Cult And Help Witches Again In Today's Biweekly Patch
item.content:encoded:
A new biweekly content update for the indie MMORPG Eterspire is on its way with new lower-level quests that expands the early game’s lore, a change in pace after seeing plenty of late-game additions over the last few months. 

New quests in this update involve delving a little more into the secrets of the Unlucky Cult, which are responsible for all of the Unlucky Coins you pick up in the early game. The “Hairless in the Cavern” quest for it will be a level 36 quest that’s a direct sequel to “Misfortune Favors the Bald.”

A few more lower-level quests also arrive in this update. In the level 17 quest “An Unfortunate Incantation,” we tackle a spider problem with Griselda the Witch, while and players help the former button-hole-making witch Irma Guswacha from the Firefly Forest region craft a new cape in “The Warrior’s Courage” at level 29. 

For the real grinders out there, there’s a new follow-up to the Slayer Cloak, which was introduced in a recent patch for taking down all of the Remnant bosses. Now, the Silver and Gold Slayer Cloaks reward beating down all of the Remnants, respectively, 250 and 500 times. 

This update arrives today, with more details now available on the official patch notes page.  Plus, according to a press email by Stonehollow, the last of the early 2026 content and improvement roadmap will be wrapping up soon, with a new roadmap for the next part of the year on the way; this likely means we’ll be seeing Guilds and Fishing skill updates introduced soon.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/learn-more-about-eterspires-unlucky-cult-and-help-witches-again-in-todays-biweekly-patch-2000137804
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Last Flag, The Capture The Flag Multiplayer Shooter By Night Street Games, Is Now Live On PC
item.content:encoded:
The debut effort from indie dev Night Street Games, Last Flag, is now live on PC, bringing its capture-the-flag shooter and its alternate '70s-inspired setting to players on Steam and the Epic Games Store. 

Night Street Games, which is co-founded by Mac and Dan Reynolds of Imagine Dragons fame, has been working on the title for a few years now with its fully remote team, with its main capture-the-flag gameplay inspired by CTF games the two Reynolds brothers would play as kids growing up. 

Now, the 5v5 CTF shooter is launching onto the scene on Steam and the Epic Games Store, with players taking part in an "alternate 1970s-inspired TV show where the larger-than-life contestants compete in the ultimate game of capture the flag."

All the action takes place on a rather detailed map, with tons of hiding places for you to place your flag and defend it, all the while you capture and hold towers at the center of the map to slowly start to reveal where the enemy flag is hidden. Personally, during my play tests, the combat has been hectic, with so much moment-to-moment action unfolding while trying to capture and hold those radar towers, and it can be a ton of white-knuckled fun when everything clicks. 

As someone who grew up playing capture-the-flag here in the Vegas desert as a Boy Scout, this game does bring me back, and I'm really looking forward to seeing how this progresses, especially if the team decides to put in night matches where the world is illuminated by moonlight and the subtle green glow of a glowstick. 

Thankfully, the barrier to entry is rather modest. You can buy Last Flag now for $11.99 (its regular price will be $14.99 after April 21st), while its support edition will cost you just an extra $4. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/last-flag-the-capture-the-flag-multiplayer-shooter-by-night-street-games-is-now-live-on-pc-2000137803
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pirate Survival Sandbox Windrose Has Sailed Into PC Early Access Today
item.content:encoded:
Windrose, the massive open-world pirate survival game from Kraken Express, has officially sailed into Early Access today, bringing its swashbuckling naval battles and survival gameplay to Steam. 

Players will be able to sail the high seas and explore three distinct biomes while they discover the world, which the team at Kraken Express states is procedurally generated, though filled with "handcrafted points of interest to discover." 

Via this morning's press release:

"Explore three diverse biomes with quests to complete, enemies to fight in souls-lite-inspired combat, and handcrafted points of interest to discover in a procedurally generated world. Take down epic bosses with an armory of unique weapons, including sabers, maces, muskets and more. Improve your reputation with various factions, trade, farm, fish and build a pirate haven of your own – and command your own vessel in thrilling naval battles with boarding actions. The high seas are yours for the taking!"

Windrose's points of interest range from handcrafted temples, dungeons, shipwrecks and more. Players will able to build their pirate how they want, with new skills, talents and gear to help hone in their individual role in their pirate crew, which can be up to eight players on a server (though the devs do recommend a crew of four players for tbe "best gameplay experience."

Windrose has launched on Steam, Epic Games Store, and Stove (literally never heard of this one) for the EA price of $29.99, but as always nowadays, you can spend a little extra on the supporter bundle to get some extra goodies, in this case the soundtrack and a wallpaper pack, for $39.99.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/pirate-survival-sandbox-windrose-has-sailed-into-pc-early-access-today-2000137801
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Update 50, and Some Season One Content, Now on The Elder Scrolls Online PTS
item.content:encoded:
The Elder Scrolls Online is now testing Update 50 (and some Season One content) on the PTS. You can help the ZeniMax team to test new features like PvP Veterancy, custom challenge difficulty levels, the new Werewolf updates, story content, and more.

The team is testing these features to release some of them in Season Zero and some in Season One. If you've ever wanted a bit more of a challenge but still wanted to play, enter Challenge Difficulty. This feature will let you set the difficulty level for most content you're playing through. You can opt into this and alter the difficulty to suit your preferences. Any selection will only apply to you, so you can  feel free to play with friends even if you are playing the same content with different difficulty settings.

PvP Veterancy  is another new feature. This is essentially a rewards track system  for PvPers.  Those who love taking on other players in Cyrodiil, Imperial City, Battlegrounds, can earn titles, consumables, unique cosmetics, and even perks for Vengeance. 

Class Mastery is also up for testing. Upon release, this will offer special passives to level 50 pure classes. Consider these an incentive to play pure classes and part of the team’s efforts to make doing so more viable following the often-dominant effect of subclassing.

In a look ahead to Season One, the PTS also features new Thieves Guild and Sheogorath questlines that will debut in that season. Thieves Guild content is on this week’s PTS build and the Sheogorath quest is coming next week.The new Favors system is also set to be tested before it launches in Season One.

Check out the PTS patch notes for details on what’s available, and this announcement for more on how to get started.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/update-50-and-some-season-one-content-now-on-the-elder-scrolls-online-pts-2000137802
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: BEACN Announces Its First Headset, Which Will Start Shipping Later This Spring
item.content:encoded:
$349 USD

BEACN has announced its first headset, the aptly named BEACN Headset, which aims to bring high-quality voice comms and polished audio to gamers, creators, or simply headphone lovers everywhere. 

I'm going to make no secret about it: I love BEACN's products. The BEACN Mic is still my preferred computer Mic of choice, and its BEACN Mix Create is a stellar audio controller for your PC, which I still use to this day alongside my Stream Deck +. 

So I'm a bit excited to see where BEACN is taking its years of audio knowledge and prowess with its first consumer headset. And based on the info detailed on the BEACN website this morning, there seems to be a lot to like here. 

First things first: the BEACN headset will feature a detachable mic, which, if you choose to use it for your streams, gaming, or ZOOM meetings, the mic should allow for the same deep voice DSP customization found already within the BEACN software suite. According to the press release this morning, the microphone uses BEACN's proprietary "real-time processing technology," which runs directly on the headset itself, meaning there is no CPU overhead on your PC. The mic is an omnidirectional capsule that is marketed as a "headset mic that actually sounds good." BEACN states that the result is a policed sound that is reminiscent of a podcast-quality mic, "not a headset."

These are a set of headphones, however, so how the cans sound is paramount. BEACN is stating that the headset is designed in a way where "no single 'ideal' works for everyone." To support this, BEACN announced a feature it's calling Binaural Personalization, which allows you to adjust and set an EQ curve on a per-ear basis. 

Via this morning's press release:

"This level of personalization only works because BEACN Headset is designed from the start to support it. Custom silk-dome drivers are paired with precisely matched acoustic cavities and a high-power amplifier capable of handling deep EQ without breaking down. This hardware foundation allows BEACN’s personalization tools to make meaningful, audible changes — not just cosmetic adjustments."

The BEACN Headset also boasts a built-in subwoofer algorithm which enhances bass, two sets earcups: a leatherette style and fabric style ear cup, both gel-cooled, and more. Since the headset does all the processing on the unit and not your PC, you individual audio tuning settings will save to the headset itself, meaning you'll take those settings and sound stage with you, whether you're gaming on your PC, PS5 or just strolling through the park on a walk listening to your favorite album. 

The BEACN Headset will retail for an introductory price of $349 USD/$399 CAD and will start shipping later this Spring. 

Joseph Bradford

Joseph has been writing or podcasting about games in some form since about 2012. Having written for multiple major outlets such as IGN, Playboy, and more, Joseph started writing for MMORPG in 2015. When he's not writing or talking about games, you can typically find him hanging out with his 15-year old or playing Magic: The Gathering with his family. Also, don't get him started on why Balrogs *don't* have wings. You can find him on Twitter @LotrLore



https://www.mmorpg.com/hardware/beacn-announces-its-first-headset-which-will-start-shipping-later-this-spring-2000137799
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
